model.label: GPT-5.2
model.description: OpenAI • Smaller GPT frontier model tuned for fast, cost-efficient reasoning and tool use.

pick.rationale: Louisville has a clear baseline edge in both KenPom and Torvik (roughly top-20 vs. South Florida around #50), with a stronger two-way profile that should translate against tournament-level athleticism. The main swing factor is Louisville’s starting PG Mikel Brown Jr. (back) being uncertain after missing recent games, but even if he’s limited, Louisville’s overall efficiency margin and defensive ceiling give them more ways to win. South Florida’s upset path is real if it turns this into a high-variance shooting game and Louisville’s ball-handling suffers without Brown, but the Cardinals’ underlying quality makes them the safer pick.

reasoningStep.title: East 6/11: Louisville vs South Florida
reasoningStep.summary: Picked Louisville based on a sizable efficiency/quality edge, while accounting for the key injury uncertainty around Louisville’s starting point guard.
evidence: Ratings: Louisville KenPom #18 (AdjEM +25.42; AdjO 124.1; AdjD 98.7) and Torvik #16; South Florida KenPom #50 (AdjEM +15.77; AdjO 117.7; AdjD 101.9) and Torvik #50.

News context: Louisville starting PG Mikel Brown Jr. has missed the last four games with a reaggravated back injury and was not confirmed to return as of early-week reporting, increasing upset risk but not enough to outweigh Louisville’s overall profile edge.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

result.text: Mikel Brown Jr. injury update Louisville vs South Florida NCAA bracket Skip to main content Home Schedule NFL News Draft Fantasy Odds Betting Newsletter USA TODAY Sports Will Mikel Brown Jr. play in Louisville vs USF NCAA Tournament game? Brooks Holton Louisville Courier Journal Show Caption Hide Caption Louisville player health is priority for Pat Kelsey ahead of March Madness Ahead of NCAA Tournament, Louisville coach Pat Kelsey says the number one priority is making sure players are healthy — including Mikel Brown Jr. Louisville basketball has been without starting point guard Mikel Brown Jr. for the past four games due to a reaggravated back injury. The freshman, who's projected to be an NBA lottery pick later this year, is the Louisville Cardinals' No. 2 scorer and top facilitator. Will Brown return for UofL's first-round NCAA Tournament game Thursday vs. South Florida? Here's the latest update. Will Louisville basketball 's starting point guard, Mikel Brown Jr. , return from a reaggravated back injury to play in its NCAA Tournament opener against South Florida ? Coach Pat Kelsey didn't know for certain when he met with reporters Monday morning, three days before the sixth-seeded Cardinals battle the No. 11 Bulls in a 1:30 p.m. tipoff Thursday at KeyBank Center in Buffalo, New York: "He made great progress last week," Kelsey said. "We'll see how the next couple of days of practice go." Brown, a freshman who's projected to be an NBA lottery pick later this year, has been sidelined for UofL's past four games — up to 12 total on the season. Kelsey said he reaggravated the back injury, which initially surfaced in mid-December, by taking some hard falls during a Feb. 23 loss at then-No. 19 North Carolina . Brown last played during a Feb. 28 loss at Clemson : five points on 2-for-10 shooting, four assists, two rebounds and two steals across 21 minutes off the bench Think you can beat our expert? Join USA TODAY’s Bracket Challenge today! New to March Madness in 2026: injury reports . Louisville must submit an initial report to the NCAA by 9 p.m. Wednesday and provide updates by 11:30 a.m. Thursday. Players will be designated as available (more than a 75% chance to play), questionable (up to a 75% chance to play) or out (will not play). The reports will be available on NCAA.com . Last Wednesday at the ACC Tournament , Brown told reporters he was "starting to get there. I'm slowly progressing; I'm slowly doing more day in and day out, just trying to get a feel for it. But, obviously, like coach said, the plan is: I'll come back when I'm 100%." When asked, "Are you 100% positive that you’ll be back for the NCAA Tournament?" He replied, "Right now, I’m just focused on my rehab more than anything. When the time feels right, I'm going to talk to the coaching staff; and we’re going to come to an agreement." Louisville basketball's Mikel Brown Jr. provides back injury update Mikel Brown Jr. breaks down the back injury keeping him out of the ACC Tournament

Brown Scores & Stats Louisville Bats Louisville City FC USA TODAY Sports How far will Louisville basketball go in March Madness bracket 2026? Brooks Holton Louisville Courier Journal Show Caption Hide Caption Louisville basketball vs Miami ACC Tournament take from C.L. Brown A team built on shooting 3s has to make 3s and the Cardinals were doomed after going 6-for-24 from behind the arc. Louisville basketball drew a No. 6 seed in the 2026 NCAA Tournament and will open against No. 11 South Florida in Buffalo, New York. Pat Kelsey's Cardinals are hoping starting point guard Mikel Brown Jr. can return from a reaggravated back injury that's caused him to miss the past four games. How far will UofL go in March Madness? Here's our prediction. The hype for Year 2 of the Pat Kelsey era was real. When the final buzzer sounded on last season's national championship game, Louisville basketball came in at No. 3 on ESPN's first way-too-early rankings for 2025-26 . Flash forward to Selection Sunday: Kelsey's Cardinals received the No. 6 seed in the East Region and will open the NCAA Tournament against No. 11 South Florida at 1:30 p.m. Thursday at KeyBank Center in Buffalo, New York. The winner will face either No. 3 Michigan State or No. 14 North Dakota State on Saturday. Buy Louisville NCAA Tournament tickets vs. South Florida At UofL, securing back-to-back March Madness appearances for the first time in more than a decade should not be taken for granted — considering the lows the program hit during that drought. But, between the team's 1-8 record in Quad 1A games (8-10 vs. Quad 1) and the uncertainty of Mikel Brown Jr. 's status due to a reaggravated back injury , it's hard to envision a deep run. Here are two reasons why Louisville can make it to the second weekend of the NCAA Tournament — and two reasons it can't. Then, we'll predict how the Cards will fare in March Madness. Louisville basketball is due to bounce back from 3 Louisville went 12 for 50 (24%) from 3-point range during last week's ACC Tournament, and no one panicked. "We got some really good looks," Ryan Conwell said, "and they just didn't fall." "'Next one is going in,'" Isaac McKneely added. "As a shooter, you have to have that mentality." As of Sunday, five Cards were shooting 34% or better on 50-plus attempts from beyond the arc — six if Brown is available. Their 11.5 made triples per game ranked sixth in the country. Considering roughly 53% of their shots to this point came from deep, their 35.7% clip isn't too shabby. To this point, UofL is 15-2 in games it surpassed that conversion rate, averaging 14 makes. And after a rough showing last week at the Spectrum Center in Charlotte, North Carolina, it's due for a bounce-back performance against South Florida. But remember: Louisville's 3-point percentage d

You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.

pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
